summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (1985-NY-Yankees) that retrieves the roster of the 1985 New York Yankees from GPT-4o via TRAPI, then persists the result to Azure Blob Storage. Authentication must use Managed Identity (no API keys). The function runs on a timer trigger scheduled nightly.
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the internal Azure OpenAI proxy/gateway that accepts GPT-4o requests and supports Managed Identity authentication.
The Azure Function will be implemented in Python using the Azure Functions v2 programming model.
Infrastructure will be provisioned using Bicep as the IaC tool.
The function will use a Consumption or Flex Consumption hosting plan unless cost/latency constraints dictate otherwise.
The Azure Storage Account for blob output will be a new dedicated account provisioned for this project.
The output blob will be JSON-formatted containing an array of player names/details.
Managed Identity (system-assigned) will be used to authenticate against both TRAPI and Azure Blob Storage.
No VNet integration is required unless specified by the TRAPI endpoint network policy.
The nightly schedule is UTC midnight (0 0 * * *) unless the team specifies otherwise.
Application Insights will be used for observability.
The spec does not explicitly auto-approve the task plan, so PM-01 requires manual approval.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and required request schema for GPT-4o?
What RBAC role or scope is required to grant the Function's Managed Identity access to TRAPI?
What is the target Azure subscription, resource group, and region?
What is the desired blob container name and output blob naming convention (e.g., fixed name overwritten nightly vs. date-stamped)?
Are there data retention or blob lifecycle management requirements?
What language/runtime is preferred (Python assumed; confirm)?
Is a specific Azure Functions hosting plan required (Consumption, Flex Consumption, Premium)?
What is the exact nightly schedule time (UTC midnight assumed)?
Are there alerting/on-call notification channels (email, Teams, PagerDuty)?
Is a staging/test environment required in addition to production?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all cloud infrastructure, identities, and secrets scaffolding needed before any application code is written.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and open questions resolved or baselined with assumptions
Azure subscription and resource group target confirmed
exit_criteria: PM-01 approved
Azure Function App, Storage Account, and Application Insights provisioned via IaC
System-assigned Managed Identity enabled and RBAC assignments applied
GitHub Actions service principal configured with least-privilege deployment permissions
Application Insights workspace active and Function App connected

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the Azure Function with timer trigger, TRAPI integration, and blob storage output.
entry_criteria: All Foundation wave exit criteria met
TRAPI endpoint URL and Managed Identity auth flow confirmed
Blob container name and output schema agreed
exit_criteria: Timer-triggered Azure Function executes nightly on schedule
Function successfully queries GPT-4o via TRAPI using Managed Identity
Full 1985 Yankees roster returned and validated
Roster written to Azure Blob Storage as JSON using Managed Identity
Unit and integration tests passing

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, error handling, observability, and security controls to production-harden the solution.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function end-to-end tested in dev/staging environment
exit_criteria: Retry and exception handling implemented and tested
No secrets or API keys present in code or configuration
Structured logging and distributed tracing active in Application Insights
Alert rules configured for function failures and missed executions
Security review checklist complete

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate the full solution in staging, complete CI/CD pipeline, and deploy to production with operational sign-off.
entry_criteria: Staging environment provisioned and mirrors production config
exit_criteria: CI/CD pipeline deploys to production on merge to main
End-to-end acceptance test passes in staging: roster retrieved and stored correctly
Nightly timer trigger verified via at least one live execution
Runbook and operational documentation complete
Production deployment confirmed healthy

tasks.title: [Task PM-01] Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Stakeholders and the delivery team review this task plan, resolve open questions, validate assumptions, and formally approve before implementation begins.
acceptance_criteria: All open questions are answered or formally deferred with documented assumptions
Task plan reviewed and signed off by owner rciapala and tech lead
TRAPI endpoint and Managed Identity auth approach confirmed
Target subscription, resource group, and region documented
Blob naming convention and output schema agreed
risks: TRAPI access or Managed Identity support may not be available, blocking INF-03 and APP-01
Unresolved open questions will delay Foundation wave

tasks.title: Provision Azure Function App and hosting plan via Bicep
tasks.description: Author and deploy Bicep templates to provision the Azure Function App (Python runtime, timer trigger support), its associated storage account for function host state, and Application Insights. Use Consumption or Flex Consumption plan.
acceptance_criteria: Bicep template deploys idempotently with no errors
Function App is reachable and reports healthy in Azure portal
Python runtime version matches project requirement
Application Insights resource created and connected to Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting
No hardcoded secrets in Bicep templates
risks: Subscription quota limits may block resource provisioning
Hosting plan choice may need revision if TRAPI has VNet requirements

tasks.title: Provision Azure Blob Storage account for roster output
tasks.description: Provision a dedicated Azure Storage Account and blob container for storing nightly Yankees roster output. Configure via Bicep. Define blob naming convention (date-stamped or fixed overwrite).
acceptance_criteria: Storage Account provisioned with standard LRS redundancy (or higher if specified)
Blob container created with correct name and private access level
Storage Account accessible only via Managed Identity (no shared key access if supported)
Bicep template is idempotent and committed to repo
risks: Blob naming convention not agreed may require schema changes post-implementation

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity and RBAC assignments
tasks.description: Enable system-assigned Managed Identity on the Function App. Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor role on the output storage account. Assign required RBAC role/scope for TRAPI access. All via Bicep roleAssignment resources.
inputs: TRAPI RBAC role and scope confirmed
outputs: Managed Identity enabled on Function App
acceptance_criteria: Function App system-assigned identity is enabled and object ID recorded
Storage Blob Data Contributor role assigned to identity on output storage account
TRAPI RBAC role assigned to identity at correct scope
No storage account keys or API keys configured as app settings
Role assignments validated by attempting token acquisition in dev environment
risks: TRAPI Managed Identity support unconfirmed — may require fallback to federated credential or alternative auth
RBAC propagation delay (up to 10 min) may cause false failures in initial testing

tasks.title: Establish CI/CD service principal with least-privilege deployment permissions
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions service principal (or use federated OIDC identity) with minimal permissions to deploy Bicep and publish the Function App. Store credentials as GitHub Actions secrets.
outputs: Service principal or federated credential created
Permissions scoped to resource group only
acceptance_criteria: CI/CD identity has Contributor role scoped to the project resource group only
No subscription-level Owner or Contributor assignments
Credentials stored as GitHub Actions encrypted secrets (not in code)
OIDC federated credential preferred over long-lived client secret
risks: Using long-lived client secrets increases credential exposure risk if OIDC is unavailable

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights and configure structured logging
tasks.description: Ensure Application Insights workspace is connected to the Function App. Configure the Python logging module to emit structured JSON logs. Define log schema for key events: function invocation, TRAPI request/response, blob write success/failure.
outputs: Structured logging configured in function host.json and application code
Key log events defined and documented
acceptance_criteria: Function invocation logs appear in Application Insights within 2 minutes of execution
Structured log events include: invocation start, TRAPI call duration, player count returned, blob write success/failure
No PII or secrets logged
host.json logging level set appropriately (Information for prod)
risks: Overly verbose logging may increase Application Insights ingestion costs

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client for GPT-4o roster query
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that authenticates to TRAPI using azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential, sends a prompt to GPT-4o requesting the full 1985 New York Yankees roster, and parses the structured response into a list of player records.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and API schema
acceptance_criteria: Client authenticates via DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity in Azure, dev credential locally) with no API keys
Prompt is structured to elicit a complete, consistent roster response from GPT-4o
Response parsed into a list of player dicts with at minimum: name, position, uniform number (where available)
Client raises a typed exception on HTTP error or empty/malformed response
Unit tests mock the TRAPI HTTP call and validate parsing logic
No API keys or credentials hardcoded
risks: GPT-4o may return incomplete or inconsistent roster data; prompt engineering and response validation required
TRAPI auth scope or endpoint may differ from standard Azure OpenAI SDK — integration testing required early

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that writes the roster data (JSON-serialized) to the designated Azure Blob Storage container using azure-storage-blob with DefaultAzureCredential. Support both date-stamped and fixed blob name strategies.
acceptance_criteria: Blob writer authenticates via DefaultAzureCredential with no connection strings or keys
Roster data serialized as valid JSON and written to correct container and blob name
Overwrites existing blob if fixed naming used; creates new blob if date-stamped
Raises typed exception on write failure
Unit tests validate serialization and mock blob client calls
risks: Blob naming convention not finalized may require rework

tasks.title: Implement nightly timer-triggered Azure Function
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function using the v2 Python programming model with a TimerTrigger set to a nightly CRON schedule. Orchestrate calls to the TRAPI client and blob writer, with structured logging at each step.
acceptance_criteria: Timer trigger CRON expression matches agreed nightly schedule
Function invokes TRAPI client, validates response, then writes to blob
Structured log events emitted at invocation start, after TRAPI call, and after blob write
Function exits with success on happy path; logs and raises on error
local.settings.json template committed with placeholder values only (no real secrets or keys)
risks: Timer drift or missed executions on Consumption plan during cold start — mitigate with runFromPackage and health monitoring

tasks.title: Write unit and integration tests
tasks.description: Author unit tests for trapi_client, blob_writer, and the function orchestration logic. Author at least one integration test that validates the end-to-end flow against the dev environment using real Azure resources.
outputs: tests/ directory with unit and integration test suites
acceptance_criteria: Unit test suite covers happy path and error/exception paths for all modules
Integration test invokes the function in dev environment and asserts blob is written with non-empty player list
Tests run successfully in CI (GitHub Actions) with mocked Azure credentials for unit tests
Test coverage at or above 80%
No secrets in test code or fixtures
risks: Integration tests require a live Azure dev environment; may be skipped in PR CI and run only on merge

tasks.title: Implement retry logic and error handling
tasks.description: Add retry logic (with exponential backoff) to TRAPI calls and blob writes. Handle transient failures gracefully. Ensure the function does not silently succeed on partial failure. Log all errors with sufficient context for debugging.
outputs: Retry and error handling logic in trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times on HTTP 429 or 5xx with exponential backoff
Blob writer retries on transient storage errors
Non-retryable errors (e.g., 401 auth failure) are logged and raised immediately without retry
Function execution marked as failed in Application Insights on unhandled exception
Retry behavior covered by unit tests using mocked HTTP responses
risks: Overly aggressive retries may cause function timeout on Consumption plan (10-min limit)

tasks.title: Security controls review and hardening
tasks.description: Conduct a security review of the implementation: verify no secrets in code/config, confirm Managed Identity is the sole auth mechanism, review RBAC assignments for least privilege, and ensure blob data is not publicly accessible.
outputs: Confirmed: no API keys, no shared access signatures in code
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning shows no secrets in repo history
No API keys, connection strings, or SAS tokens present in code, app settings, or config files
Storage Account has 'Allow Blob Public Access' disabled
RBAC assignments follow least privilege (Storage Blob Data Contributor, not Owner/Contributor)
Managed Identity is the only auth method for both TRAPI and Blob Storage
risks: TRAPI may not support Managed Identity, requiring escalation before this task can close

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights alerts for function failures
tasks.description: Create Azure Monitor alert rules to notify the team when the nightly function fails or does not execute within the expected window. Define notification channel (email, Teams webhook, or PagerDuty).
acceptance_criteria: Alert fires within 5 minutes of a function exception or failed invocation
Missed execution alert fires if function has not run within 26 hours of last expected execution
Alert notifications delivered to agreed channel and acknowledged in test
Alert rules deployed as IaC (Bicep) not manually configured
risks: Notification channel not confirmed — alert delivery cannot be validated until confirmed

tasks.title: Author operational runbook
tasks.description: Write a runbook documenting: how to manually trigger the function, how to verify blob output, how to diagnose TRAPI or storage failures, how to rotate or update identity permissions, and escalation contacts.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook covers: manual invocation, output verification, failure diagnosis, identity/RBAC update procedure
Escalation contacts and on-call procedure documented
Runbook reviewed by at least one team member other than the author
docs/runbook.md committed to repo main branch
risks: Runbook may be deprioritized; incomplete ops docs increase MTTR on incidents

tasks.title: Implement GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
tasks.description: Implement GitHub Actions workflows for: (1) PR validation — run unit tests and linting; (2) deploy-to-production — deploy Bicep IaC and publish Function App on merge to main using the SEC-01 identity.
outputs: Deployment validated in GitHub Actions run
acceptance_criteria: PR workflow runs unit tests and fails PR if tests fail
Deploy workflow runs on merge to main and deploys Bicep then publishes function package
No secrets hardcoded in workflow files
Deployment uses OIDC federated credential or GitHub Actions secret (no plaintext credentials)
Successful end-to-end deployment confirmed via GitHub Actions run log
risks: OIDC federated credential setup may require Azure AD configuration permissions not held by the team

tasks.title: End-to-end acceptance testing in staging
tasks.description: Execute end-to-end acceptance tests in a staging environment that mirrors production. Manually trigger the function, verify the GPT-4o response contains a plausible 1985 Yankees roster, and confirm the JSON blob is written correctly to storage.
inputs: Access to staging Application Insights and Storage Account
outputs: Blob output reviewed and roster completeness validated
acceptance_criteria: Function invoked manually in staging completes without error
Blob written to storage contains a JSON array of at least 25 players
Player records include at minimum: name and position
Application Insights shows successful invocation with structured logs
No secrets or API keys observed in any config, log output, or blob content
Owner rciapala reviews and approves blob output
risks: GPT-4o may return variable roster completeness across invocations — may require prompt refinement

tasks.title: Production deployment and go-live verification
tasks.description: Deploy the solution to production via the CI/CD pipeline. Verify the nightly timer is active, confirm the first live execution succeeds, and validate the blob output and Application Insights telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Production Function App deployed via CI/CD pipeline (not manual)
Timer trigger active and scheduled for nightly execution confirmed in Azure portal
First live execution completes successfully: blob written with full roster
Application Insights telemetry confirms successful invocation
Alert rules active and notification channel confirmed live
rciapala provides go-live sign-off
risks: First live execution may be delayed by up to 24 hours if deployed mid-day — consider manual trigger for initial validation

tasks.title: Complete project documentation and README
tasks.description: Write a README.md covering: project purpose, local development setup, environment variable requirements (with no real values), how to deploy, how to run tests, and links to the runbook.
acceptance_criteria: README covers local dev setup, environment variables (placeholders only), deployment instructions, and test execution
Link to docs/runbook.md included
No secrets or real credential values present in README
README reviewed by at least one team member
risks: Documentation deprioritized under delivery pressure — include in CI-01 PR gate
